input.prompt:
I have a custom attribute that inherits from TestMethodAttribute with a constructor
that takes a string parameter for display name. After upgrading to MSTest v4, the
base constructor signature changed. How do I fix this?

data.reasoningText:
Let me understand the issue. In MSTest v4, `TestMethodAttribute` changed its constructor signature. The user's `CategorizedTestMethodAttribute` calls the parameterless base constructor `TestMethodAttribute()`, which may no longer exist or has changed in v4.

Let me first upgrade to MSTest v4 and see what the actual error is.

data.reasoningText:
The build succeeds but with warnings. The key warnings are:

1. **MSTEST0057**: `TestMethodAttribute` derived class `CategorizedTestMethodAttribute` should add `CallerFilePath` and `CallerLineNumber` parameters to its constructor.
2. **MSTEST0056**: Use the `DisplayName` property instead of passing a string argument to `TestMethodAttribute`.

In MSTest v4, the `TestMethodAttribute` constructor now accepts `CallerFilePath` and `CallerLineNumber` parameters, and the string-parameter constructor is deprecated in favor of the `DisplayName` property.

Let me fix both issues:


I need to update `CategorizedTestMethodAttribute` to include the caller information parameters and pass them to the base constructor, and then replace any string-based test method declarations with the `DisplayName` property approach. Let me check the exact constructor signature for `TestMethodAttribute` in MSTest v4 to make sure I get this right.

<member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute">
            <summary>
            This attribute doesn't currently provide any different functionality compared to <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/>. It's only
            present for backward compatibility. Using <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/> is recommended, even for parameterized tests.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute"/> class.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DynamicDataSourceType">
            <summary>
            Enum to specify whether the data is stored as property, in method, or in field.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DynamicDataSourceType.Property">
            <summary>
            Data is declared as property.
            </summary>
        </member>

            <summary>
            Data is declared in method.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DynamicDataSourceType.AutoDetect">
            <summary>
            The data source type is auto-detected.
--
            The test cleanup attribute marks methods that are executed after every test marked with a <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DoNotParallelizeAttribute">
            <summary>
            Specification to disable parallelization.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ExecutionScope">
            <summary>
            Parallel execution mode.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ExecutionScope.ClassLevel">
            <summary>
            Each thread of execution will be handed a TestClass worth of tests to execute.
            Within the TestClass, the test methods will execute serially.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ExecutionScope.MethodLevel">
            <summary>
--
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestClassAttribute.GetTestMethodAttribute(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute)">
            <inheritdoc />
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute">
            <summary>

            </summary>
        </member>
<exited with exit code 0>

            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute"/> class.
            This constructor is intended to be called by <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestClassAttribute"/> (or derived) to wrap an existing test method attribute.
            It can also be called by a derived <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute"/> which will be called by a derived <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestClassAttribute"/>.
            </summary>
            <param name="testMethodAttribute">The wrapped test method.</param>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute.UseSTASynchronizationContext">
            <summary>
             Gets or sets a value indicating whether the attribute will set a <see cref="T:System.Threading.SynchronizationContext"/> that preserves the same
             STA thread for async continuations.
             The default is <see langword="false"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute.ExecuteCoreAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ITestMethod)">
--

        </member>

data.content: Now I can see the MSTest v4 changes. The `TestMethodAttribute` constructor now takes `CallerFilePath` and `CallerLineNumber` instead of a display name string. The display name is now a property. Let me fix both issues:
data.reasoningText:
So in MSTest v4, the `TestMethodAttribute` constructor signature is `#ctor(System.String, System.Int32)` - which takes a `string` (CallerFilePath) and `int` (CallerLineNumber). The old `string` parameter for display name has been replaced with these caller info parameters, and `DisplayName` is now a separate property.

Let me look at the warning messages more carefully:
- **MSTEST0057**: The derived class should add `CallerFilePath` and `CallerLineNumber` parameters to its constructor
- **MSTEST0056**: Use `DisplayName` property instead of string argument to `TestMethodAttribute`


The constructor parameters are decorated with `[CallerFilePath]` and `[CallerLineNumber]` attributes with default values, so I need to update `CategorizedTestMethodAttribute` to accept and forward these parameters to the base constructor, and change any attribute usages that pass a display name as a string argument to use the `DisplayName` property instead.

toolCalls.name: edit
bash

data.content:
Build succeeds with no MSTEST warnings and all tests pass. Here's what changed:

**Two fixes for MSTest v4:**

1. **`CategorizedTestMethodAttribute`** — Added `[CallerFilePath]` and `[CallerLineNumber]` parameters to the constructor, forwarding them to `base(filePath, lineNumber)`. MSTest v4 uses these for source location tracking (MSTEST0057).

2. **`[TestMethod("My custom display name")]`** — Changed to `[TestMethod(DisplayName = "My custom display name")]`. The string constructor parameter is now reserved for `CallerFilePath`, so display names must use the `DisplayName` property (MSTEST0056).
